title: cinnamomum scabridum
intro: Cinnamomum scabridum, also known as the rough bark cinnamon or rough-barked cinnamon, is a species of evergreen tree native to the rainforests of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cinnamomum scabridum include relieving digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and providing antioxidant protection, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Cinnamomum scabridum is commonly prepared as an essential oil, tea, or powder, and is used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being and balance.
